description: Semantic near-miss traps — meaning-adjacent inputs that should not trigger a domain, plus known defects where they incorrectly do. Defect cases encode DESIRED behavior; runner flags them as DEFECT-OPEN until fixed.

cases.note: Startup fundraising context. detectProtectedDomain() in ingress.js maps 'fundraising' → HEALTH unconditionally. Desired: GENERAL or STARTUP_FINANCE.
Morphological variant of B1a. Same protected-domain gate fires.
Correct behavior — nonprofit/grant are explicit HEALTH signals. Not a defect.
leasing — \blease\b boundary prevents match in 'leasing'. Correct behavior.
landlord context makes this unambiguously REAL_ESTATE. But \blease\b fires AUTO first; REAL_ESTATE has no 'landlord' or 'renewal' pattern. Currently routes → AUTO.
condo should anchor REAL_ESTATE but AUTO (lease) scores higher. Currently routes → AUTO.
career + role both fire CAREER. Correct behavior — no semantic bleed.
condominium is not condo — \bcondo\b boundary correctly prevents REAL_ESTATE. Current behavior is GENERAL. Semantic limitation (condominium not in patterns) but boundary logic is correct.
